notes: Fey Faire (Vol 7 slowstick #3) · fey encounter catalog.  Every fey has a Court alignment, combat stats + damage-type keyed to court, and · specific to this game · a THEME-PARK MANIFESTATION describing exactly how they present at the Faire midway (a booth, an attraction, a role) before combat begins.  Combat happens either at the booth itself (aggressive encounter) or after crossing the mirror they're behind (Fairyland encounter · they're bigger there).  Negotiation data (prize / true_name / favorite_play / request) lets the player recruit via OFFER / THREATEN / RECITE / PROMISE branches.

schema_notes.damage_type: iron | bone | flame | song | salt | word
schema_notes.manifestation: specific booth / attraction / role at the Faire · always concrete
schema_notes.true_form: what they look like when the glamour drops · Fairyland form

feys.name: Peaseblossom
feys.shakespeare_source: A Midsummer Night's Dream · Act III Sc I
feys.manifestation: the flower stall halfway down the midway · glass vases of impossibly-blue lupines, roses that smell like something other than roses.  Two dollars a stem, one dollar if you smile.  Peaseblossom is the girl behind the counter · seven years old and speaking in complete sentences.
feys.true_form: a bouquet-sized fey with pea-pod green skin, a face made of flower petals, and hands too large for her arms.  Height when standing on the counter, waist-high on the counter itself in the midway.
feys.description: Cheerful.  Genuinely.  She wants you to leave the Faire happy · that's her stake in it.  Do not underestimate what she'll pay for that.
feys.prize: any freshly-picked flower · a dandelion works
feys.request: PROMISE to plant something you'll water for a year
feys.recruit_gift: one healing skill (BLOOM_HEAL) available to protagonist

feys.name: Mustardseed
feys.shakespeare_source: A Midsummer Night's Dream · Act III Sc I
feys.manifestation: the pepper-mill booth · you pay a quarter and a small boy grinds fresh pepper into a paper cone which you carry away and use up nothing.  A single sneeze cures headaches.  The pepper-mill has been in this family (which family) for four generations.
feys.true_form: a small ochre fey the size of a hand-mustard-jar, with a face permanently mid-sneeze.
feys.description: Kind but easily set off.  Every strong smell reminds him of someone he lost.  He will not talk about it.
feys.prize: a jar of anything ground fine · turmeric, cinnamon, sea salt
feys.request: PROMISE to name your first pet after him
feys.recruit_gift: FIRE_BURST (Flame, single-target) available to protagonist

feys.name: Cobweb
feys.shakespeare_source: A Midsummer Night's Dream · Act III Sc I
feys.manifestation: the test-your-strength booth · a wooden tower with a bell at the top.  Cobweb runs it · she's ninety-three or she's thirteen, depending on the light.  She hands you the mallet and says 'do not swing hard; swing TRUE.'  The mallet is heavier than it looks.  Nobody has rung the bell this week.
feys.true_form: a fey woven of silver spider-silk with too many joints in her arms.  She has never once bitten anyone.
feys.description: Patient the way weavers are patient.  Watching you carefully.  Enjoys a game played well more than a game won.
feys.prize: a used bookmark · felt, silk, cardboard, any
feys.request: PROMISE to finish a book you started and set down
feys.recruit_gift: SILK_BIND (Song, single-target immobilize) available

feys.name: Moth
feys.shakespeare_source: A Midsummer Night's Dream · Act III Sc I
feys.manifestation: a candlelit tent that only opens after 10 PM · sign reads MOTH'S QUIET.  Inside, forty candles burning in specific patterns.  Sit on a stool for two dollars.  Moth doesn't speak; she puts a moth on your open palm.  Wingbeat spells your first honest wish.  You leave without saying it aloud.
feys.true_form: a girl-shaped fey with luna-moth wings, in size somewhere between a child and a mid-size adult.  Eyes reflect green.
feys.description: Silent.  Kind in the specific way silent people can be.  Reads people fast.  Judges rarely.
feys.prize: a lit candle · white, unscented
feys.request: PROMISE not to say your wish out loud
feys.recruit_gift: MOTH_CHARM (Song, single-target charmed)

feys.name: Ondine
feys.manifestation: the ring-toss booth at the Gate · the FIRST booth you meet.  Cream and mauve stripes.  Ondine is nineteen years old with hair the color of salt-washed rope.  Behind her, plush prizes hanging in a line, none of which anyone has won in living memory.  She hands you three rings.  She says 'You get six nights.'  This is not a metaphor.
feys.true_form: a fey the size of a young woman, with skin that holds seawater under it (visibly, if you look closely).  Her hair drips when she is upset.  She doesn't get upset often.
feys.description: The first daughter of a fey sea-lord who is not present.  She works the ring-toss because someone has to be the door.  Old · older than she looks.  Has a good singing voice and won't demonstrate.
feys.prize: a used seashell (unbroken)
feys.request: PROMISE to visit a beach within the next year
feys.recruit_gift: SALT_LASH (Salt, single-target medium damage) + one free negotiation reset per run

feys.name: Ariel
feys.manifestation: the Ferris wheel operator · Ariel wears a striped coat and speaks with the wind at his back always.  When the wheel is running, the wind at the top of it is the wrong direction for the weather.  Riders sometimes hear their own name at the peak · always in Ariel's voice.  He does not ride.
feys.true_form: an air-fey the shape of a slim young man in his twenties, translucent at the edges.  Where he touches solid ground the ground darkens.
feys.description: Currently in service · to whom, exactly, is unclear.  A pact he made long enough ago that the phrasing is archaic.  Wants free.  Will not say so.
feys.prize: a small hand-carved wooden object · not iron, not bought
feys.true_name: Ariel (it is his true name and he tells you if you ask)
feys.request: PROMISE to release something you own that was given to you as an obligation
feys.recruit_gift: STORM_CALL (Song, all-enemies medium) + BOSS-KEY to Mirror 2

feys.name: Titania
feys.manifestation: The House of Roses · a glass conservatory behind the Big Top, only visible on nights 2-6.  Every rose inside is a different variety, some real, some not.  Titania sits at a small table having tea with no one.  A second cup is always poured.  She looks over her shoulder when you enter.  She's been expecting you.
feys.true_form: a woman-shaped fey at exactly her most-imposing height, dressed in living petals that reorganize when she moves.  Larger in Fairyland (the rose garden dungeon expands to fit her).
feys.description: Queen of the summer court, but not here in that capacity · she's here to interview YOU.  Her son (also here, elsewhere) is a source of ongoing conflict with her husband.  She wants an ally against Oberon.  Will not say so plainly.  Speaks in complete sentences that all mean two things at once.
feys.prize: a rose you did not buy · picked or received as a gift
feys.request: PROMISE to remain loyal to her against Oberon at the closing night
feys.recruit_gift: GLAMOUR_CHARM (Song, all-enemies charmed for 1 round) + court alignment locks seelie

feys.name: Oberon
feys.manifestation: backstage at the Big Top · not accessible until Night 4.  Oberon holds court on a plywood platform painted to look like an oak throne.  Six lesser Unseelie feys in attendance who will not look at you.  He is negotiating a treaty when you enter · with whom is off-stage.  He turns and smiles.  He is not smiling.
feys.true_form: a man-shaped fey seated at the exact height needed to look down at whoever is standing.  Antlers small enough to be a crown.  Skin the specific green-black of ivy that has strangled a tree.
feys.description: King of the shadow court.  Currently in an argument with his wife.  Would like the changeling that argument is about.  Would also like YOU · as an ally, an asset, or an amusement, depending on how you approach him.
feys.prize: something you took but weren't supposed to · petty theft counts
feys.request: PROMISE to name your ally against him falsely
feys.recruit_gift: COMMANDING_BONE (Bone, all-enemies heavy) + court alignment locks unseelie

feys.name: Puck (Robin Goodfellow)
feys.manifestation: the coin-in-a-glass booth · an old man with too many teeth demonstrating that if you slide the quarter down the ramp JUST SO it lands in the shot glass.  Two dollars for three tries.  Nobody wins.  Halfway through your first try Puck asks your name, which is fine, but he asks it TWICE, which is not.
feys.true_form: a slim shape-shifter with a mocking mouth and hair the wrong length depending on which side you look from.  Under three feet tall or exactly your height.  Never in between.
feys.description: A mercurial trickster.  Unusually good-natured for a fey who could kill you casually.  His interest in you is genuine but not necessarily aligned with your survival.  Loves a good story more than he loves any specific person.
feys.prize: a joke that lands · you must actually tell it and Puck must actually laugh
feys.favorite_play: A Midsummer Night's Dream (specifically the epilogue)
feys.request: PROMISE to break a specific rule at least once in the next year
feys.recruit_gift: FOOL_YOU (Word, reassigns enemy damage types randomly for 3 rounds)

feys.name: The Kelpie
feys.manifestation: the drinking fountain at the far end of the midway · shaped like a horse's head with water pouring from its mouth.  It is not connected to any pipe.  The water is very cold and very clean and tastes vaguely of iron.  There is a hair (a horse-hair) at the bottom of the basin.  Do not drink if you already had a drink today.
feys.true_form: a black horse with kelp for a mane, wet always, tall.  Human-form is a wet-haired man missing all his teeth.  Both forms will drown you.
feys.description: Old.  Hungry the way rivers are hungry.  Not malicious, exactly · this is what he does.  Respects a lure well-cast.  Once you're on his back the deal is done.
feys.prize: an actual iron horseshoe · you can find one at the Bookstall for four gold
feys.request: PROMISE to drink from no fey water for a year and a day
feys.recruit_gift: DROWNING_CHARGE (Salt, single-target heavy)

feys.name: The Selkie
feys.manifestation: the fishbowl booth · win a goldfish (real, live, in a plastic bag with too little water) by tossing a ping-pong ball into the right jar.  The Selkie is the booth operator, a woman in her forties with long dark hair kept damp deliberately.  Every fish she gives you asks a question in your head on the walk home.
feys.true_form: in seal-form, a common grey seal.  In human form, a woman between 30 and 50 depending on how she chooses today.  Her seal-skin is folded in a bureau drawer at the back of the booth.  It is not what you think you can steal.
feys.description: Currently free · her skin is hers, not a lover's.  She's here because she likes the Faire.  She likes conversations about honesty.  She does not lie, but she omits.
feys.prize: a smooth stone you carried from home in your pocket · not one you picked up here
feys.request: PROMISE never to tell someone else's secret to a third person for seven years
feys.recruit_gift: SEVEN_YEAR_SWAP (utility · trade one HP for one SP permanently, one-time)

feys.name: The Boggart
feys.manifestation: the lost-and-found booth · a plywood shed painted with a smiling face that has too many teeth.  The Boggart is behind the counter, wearing a name tag that says NOT LOOKING AT YOU.  Every item in the lost-and-found is real and lost by someone.  You may take one.  You will owe him something equally weighted.
feys.true_form: a small hairy fey shaped like a very grumpy raccoon standing on its hind legs.  Fond of hats.
feys.description: A house-spirit that went wrong · long ago someone insulted him and he never let it go.  Petty, ambitious in small ways.  Wants respect he isn't given.  Will fight dirty if provoked.
feys.prize: a small courtesy · say 'thank you, sir' unprompted at the moment of trade
feys.request: PROMISE to apologize to one specific person you wronged (they know who)
feys.recruit_gift: PETTY_CURSE (Iron, single-target minor)

feys.name: The Pooka
feys.manifestation: the funhouse mirror maze · nine mirrors that don't show what they should.  The Pooka is the attendant, wearing a rabbit costume that is (obviously to you, unlisted to everyone else) not a costume.  He rents you a token for a dollar.  Some mirrors show you a person you have not yet met.
feys.true_form: a shape-shifter that prefers rabbit or horse form · black fur, red eyes.  In human form: an ambiguously-aged white-haired man who moves oddly.
feys.description: Playful, unpredictable, deeply not a threat unless you insult him.  Adaptive · his resistance profile shifts each turn in combat.  Enjoys watching mortals figure things out.
feys.prize: a copper coin (a real one · a 1974 penny counts double)
feys.favorite_play: A Midsummer Night's Dream (identifies with Bottom)
feys.request: PROMISE to take a Route 6 walk home at night before this month is out
feys.recruit_gift: ADAPTIVE_WARD (utility · resist type shifts to enemy's last attack each round)

feys.name: The Green Man
feys.manifestation: the cotton-candy wagon · a man in a paper hat and green overalls.  The wagon is small.  Nobody notices it isn't hooked to a truck.  Cotton candy costs a dollar and stays with you longer than cotton candy should · you'll be thinking about the taste in three days.  He is called just GREEN by the other vendors.  Do not eat any part of the cotton candy that isn't pink.
feys.true_form: a fey the shape of a broad-shouldered man made entirely of moss, ivy, brambles, and rotting oak wood.  Height variable · taller in summer, ankle-height in winter.  Voice is the sound of a forest floor.
feys.description: Older than the other feys · older than Oberon, older than the concept of a court.  Not a Shakespeare figure · Rocha inserted him from the older English folk tradition on purpose.  Neutral toward humans in general but interested in this specific one.
feys.prize: a green leaf you picked yourself and dried between the pages of a book
feys.true_name: he doesn't have one · attempting THREATEN backfires
feys.favorite_play: not Shakespeare · the anonymous Mummer's play 'St. George and the Turkish Knight'
feys.request: PROMISE to plant a tree · not a decorative one · a real one that will outlive you
feys.recruit_gift: SEASONAL_SHIFT (utility · your damage type cycles by round · unlocks all six types)

feys.name: The Redcap
feys.manifestation: the milk bottles booth · knock three plastic bottles off a table with a baseball.  The Redcap runs it wearing a red knitted cap and a broad polite smile.  His teeth are yellow.  The bottles are real.  The bat is heavier than baseballs are.  When you win (you can win here) he shakes your hand.  His hand is very warm.
feys.true_form: a compact and muscular fey the size of a stocky man, with iron-shod boots and an actual bloody cap.  The cap is red because it is refreshed regularly.  You do not want to be there for the refreshing.
feys.description: Murderous by tradition · he does not want to murder specifically YOU but the tradition holds.  Actually a good sport at fair play.  Respects a well-thrown ball.  Will not attack an unarmed opponent (that's the rule of his kind).  Will attack if you insult his cap.
feys.prize: a bottle of high-quality dark ale · cheap doesn't work
feys.request: PROMISE to win one fight this year, cleanly, with rules
feys.recruit_gift: IRON_BOOT_STOMP (Iron, single-target heavy)

feys.name: The Sluagh
feys.manifestation: the fortune-teller's booth · a small tent draped in black velvet, a cracked crystal ball, an old woman shuffling cards nobody is dealt.  She is not the Sluagh.  She works for the Sluagh.  She waves you off with a fee-quote you can't afford.  If you can afford it (twelve dollars) she reads you a card from the deck.  Every card is the same card.  The Sluagh is on the reverse of it.
feys.true_form: not a single fey · a HOST · dozens of small hunched shapes flying with the west wind, faces obscured by cloth.  The unbound restless dead of a bad harvest.
feys.description: Hunts by air, at night, from the west.  Doesn't want you specifically until nights 4-6 · then it wants you very much.  Does not negotiate easily · you're negotiating with a mob, and the mob has one opinion.  The one true name it responds to is the name of any dead ancestor of yours.
feys.prize: a folded piece of paper on which you have written the name of a specific dead person
feys.true_name: any actual dead person's name from your family · the game asks in the boot questionnaire
feys.request: PROMISE to visit the grave you named
feys.recruit_gift: SWARM_ASSAULT (Bone, all-enemies medium) · the Sluagh joins as a summonable

feys.name: The Erlking
feys.manifestation: the wheel of fortune · a tall painted wheel with sixteen sectors, each promising something specific.  The Erlking spins it.  He is tall, gaunt, in a long grey coat.  His crown is antler.  He does not raise his voice.  Nobody wins Sector 16.  Sector 16 says CHILD.
feys.true_form: a hunter-fey on a black horse, followed by a hunt of pale hounds.  The horse's saddle is empty and full of shadow.
feys.description: Not from Shakespeare · from Goethe's poem 'Der Erlkönig', which Rocha rendered directly into the Faire.  Hunts children away from their fathers.  Currently on his lunch break.  Will resume his hunt at midnight of night 5.
feys.prize: a father's promise, spoken aloud · you must literally say 'I promise' with a father in mind
feys.request: PROMISE to hear a child out for ten minutes without interrupting
feys.recruit_gift: HUNTER_GALLOP (Bone, single-target massive · risky · costs 20 SP)

feys.name: The Nuckelavee
feys.manifestation: the dunk tank · a tall glass box of dubious water, a man on a bench above it in swim trunks, a pitching lane, a target.  The Nuckelavee is the man on the bench.  He does not blink.  When he is dunked the water spills.  Where the water spills, the paint on the boards flakes.  The pitching lane is closed for repair · Nuckelavee will unblock it for you if you talk to him.
feys.true_form: a fey with the body of a horse and a rider fused to it, both skinned.  Its breath is a plague.  Salt-water is its ONLY vulnerability.
feys.description: The most-feared fey of the Orkney tradition.  Sick with itself.  Doesn't want to talk to you, exactly, but wants to sit near you a while.  Do not eat here.  Do not touch anything wet in this booth.
feys.prize: a small vial of fresh seawater
feys.true_name: he refuses his name · THREATEN doesn't work
feys.request: PROMISE to visit the sea and NOT touch it
feys.recruit_gift: PLAGUE_BREATH (Bone, all-enemies status-effect debuff)

feys.name: The Baobhan Sìth
feys.manifestation: the kissing booth · deliberately old-fashioned, deliberately gothic.  She is at the counter in a long dark dress that touches the floor.  Sign reads ONE DOLLAR · ONE KISS · ONE QUESTION.  The kiss is real.  The question is REAL.  She uses your answer.
feys.true_form: a Scottish fey shaped like a woman with green skin and deer's hooves hidden under her skirt.  Long fingernails.  Never seen without a red dress underneath the black.
feys.description: Predatory in an old-fashioned way.  Hunts men, historically · she is unconvinced you're not one.  Charming when she wants to be.  Charm is her weapon; iron is her fear.
feys.prize: a hairpin made of iron (she flinches, but accepts)
feys.true_name: Bansidhe (a specific one · she'll flinch at any Scottish surname you get right)
feys.request: PROMISE to stop wearing something red for the next month
feys.recruit_gift: GREEN_GOWN_CHARM (Bone, all-enemies charmed for 1 round)

feys.name: Queen Mab
feys.manifestation: the sleep tent · behind the funhouse, striped in mauve and cream but darker · almost black-mauve.  Sign reads DREAM FOR TWO DOLLARS · WAKE UP OR DON'T.  Mab is not visible; a bed is.  Fine linens.  You will not encounter her on the midway.  She meets you in a dream inside YOUR OWN BEDROOM · which the game rendered from the boot questionnaire's description of it.
feys.true_form: smaller than an agate, if seen directly · a fey drawn by insect wings.  In dream-space she is exactly the height needed to talk to you comfortably.  Voice is your own voice at three years old.
feys.description: Rides dreamers.  Currently interested in the specific dreams of the protagonist · what they hope for, what they fear.  She is not malicious but her currency is dreams and if you owe her a dream she will take a real one from your future.
feys.prize: a specific dream · you must describe one you actually had and stand by it
feys.true_name: Mab (open, but you have to say it in dream-space, not the midway)
feys.request: PROMISE to write down the next dream you remember, in a specific notebook
feys.recruit_gift: DREAM_RIDE (Word, target moves to your side for one round) · unlocks a NG+ bonus

feys.name: Caliban
feys.manifestation: the strongman challenge · a wide bench in an open plywood shack, plus a chain, a padlock, a metal plate.  Caliban is the strongman · shirtless, broad, hunched.  He does not speak clearly.  The sign says LIFT FIFTY POUNDS ONE-HANDED · FIVE DOLLARS · WIN A PRIZE.  The prize is a small carved wooden figure that everyone who wins throws away by the following morning.
feys.true_form: a fey the shape of a hunched, powerful, disfigured man, Prospero's servant no more.  Fluent when he chooses to be · monosyllabic when watched.  Angry, and reasonably so.
feys.description: Recently freed of a specific master.  Has come to the Faire to find a new one, or find none at all.  Will offer to serve YOU if you win the strongman challenge fairly (which requires either high strike, an iron item, or PLEAD).  You should probably not accept.
feys.prize: a book · any book, but he prefers philosophy
feys.true_name: he tells you if you win the strongman challenge without cheating
feys.request: PROMISE never to command anyone against their will · not even in a game
feys.recruit_gift: MOUNTAINOUS_STRIKE (Bone, single-target massive) · BUT court alignment gains +2 unseelie whether you accept or not

feys.name: The Leanan Sídhe
feys.manifestation: the poetry stall · a booth with a sign reading YOUR FUTURE IN VERSE · TWO DOLLARS.  She is stunning in a specific painful way.  She writes on the spot with a fountain pen.  What she writes is a poem about your life · usually good.  Sometimes very good.  If you accept it and take it home, one of two things happens.
feys.true_form: a Muse of the older kind · she is a fey whose purpose is to make artists into geniuses at the specific cost of their lives.  Beautiful, gaunt, ink-stained fingers.
feys.description: Trades brilliance for years.  Will genuinely make the recipient's remaining life extraordinary · then take the rest of it.  Aware of what she does.  Sad about it.  Would like a companion who doesn't have to die.  YOU do not have to die if you say no.  If you say yes, she'll take the deal seriously.
feys.prize: a poem you wrote yourself and haven't shown anyone
feys.true_name: she gives it to you willingly · Aisling
feys.request: PROMISE to keep writing after this Faire · specifically to keep writing badly if that's what it takes
feys.recruit_gift: MUSE_TOUCH (Word, buffs one party member's strike + wit for full run)

feys.name: A Nixie
feys.manifestation: the fountain-jumping game · a shallow pool of pennies, a wooden frog cutout painted green, a metal plunger you step on to launch a wooden lily-pad.  Land it near the frog and you win a rubber lizard.  The Nixie is not the operator; there is no operator.  Every third jump the water speaks your name back.
feys.true_form: a small water-sprite in the shape of a slim girl with too-large eyes.  Nixies are always plural in the Faire · these are Tier-1 sisters of Ondine.
feys.description: Younger, unformed.  Curious.  Not dangerous unless startled.
feys.prize: a shiny coin (a nickel is fine)
feys.true_name: Undine (any Undine works, they're a legion)
feys.request: PROMISE to drink a full glass of water first thing each morning for a week
feys.recruit_gift: small SP restore per turn if in party

feys.name: Will-o'-the-Wisp
feys.manifestation: not a booth · a light on the Edison string of lamps that isn't wired to anything.  You'll notice it moving between bulbs.  Follow it and you'll be in the wrong part of the Faire, but a part that's real.  Do NOT follow it past the Gate at night.
feys.true_form: a small floating flame the color of stagnant water.  Not warm.
feys.description: Wants to be followed.  Doesn't have a clear plan for what happens next.  Neither malicious nor kind · just committed.
feys.prize: a lit match · you have to actually light it in front of him
feys.request: PROMISE not to follow strangers offering shortcuts
feys.recruit_gift: MISLEAD (utility · one enemy attacks a random target instead of you next round)

feys.name: A Hob
feys.manifestation: the popcorn wagon · a small hairy fellow in overalls, popping corn in a tall glass box that gets full and never empties.  A dollar a bag.  Every bag has one popped kernel that's the color of blood.  It is not blood.  It is beetroot juice from his personal recipe.  Do not point it out.
feys.true_form: a hobgoblin · house-brownie's less-domesticated cousin · about knee-high, dark-eyed, always chewing.
feys.description: Cheerful.  Hungry.  Sensitive about a specific past he won't talk about (he was fired from a manor house for eating too much bread).
feys.prize: a slice of very good bread (from the Bakery, not the Bookstall)
feys.request: PROMISE to leave a heel of bread out on your kitchen counter one night · not for a mouse
feys.recruit_gift: small HP restore per turn if in party

feys.name: Cricket
feys.manifestation: the Gate ticket-taker · a small green fey the exact size of an actual cricket, sitting on a stool on top of the Gate counter.  Speaks in a chirp that resolves in your head as sentences.  Takes your ticket by biting the corner off.  Does not give change.  Cricket is the FIRST voice you hear at the Faire and the LAST.
feys.true_form: an actual cricket-sized fey · six legs, iridescent green wings, three eyes.  When she speaks a person's name she picks up two more eyes.
feys.description: Ancient, cheerful, extremely tired.  Has been at this gate for longer than the Faire has been at this town.  Would like to retire.  Is auditioning replacements silently.  Watches how you enter.  Notices what you're carrying.
feys.prize: a ticket you haven't used yet · from any other event, any year
feys.request: PROMISE to be on time for something you have been late for repeatedly
feys.recruit_gift: GATEKEEPER_CHIRP (Song, all-enemies dizzy for 1 round) · Cricket also enables the BECOME_THE_FAIRE ending path

feys.name: The Salt Sisters
feys.manifestation: the fried-dough booth · three women (identical triplets, or a fey trick) at a counter dusting fresh funnel cakes with powdered sugar and something else, imperceptible.  A dollar-fifty each.  The 'something else' is salt from a specific coastline none of you have visited.  It's fine.
feys.true_form: three small salt-fey, each about doll-height, indistinguishable.  Speak in unison always.  Sisters not in a family sense but in a functional one.
feys.description: Talkative in unison.  Motherly · unclear whose mother.  Read the salt on your food to tell you something specific about your immediate future (5 minutes ahead).
feys.prize: a fresh egg (a real one · not fey)
feys.favorite_play: King Lear (the Fool's line about salt)
feys.request: PROMISE to eat one home-cooked meal a week for the next month
feys.recruit_gift: SALT_READ (utility · reveals enemy weakness immediately in combat)

feys.name: An Ossory
feys.manifestation: the shooting gallery · pop-guns and cork bullets, targets shaped like ducks and rabbits and, unsettlingly, one grey wolf.  The Ossory is the wolf target · you cannot HIT it (the corks bounce off) but every night you play, it moves closer to the front of the row.  The gallery operator (a human) doesn't notice.
feys.true_form: an Irish curse-wolf · a man cursed by a saint (in this game, by a specific medieval Bishop of Ossory) to be a wolf for seven years.  Speaks English clearly.  Has been a wolf for four years and change.

feys.prize: a Communion wafer (deliberately not gettable at the Faire · the player must bring one from outside)
feys.request: PROMISE to check in on a friend you've lost touch with, this week
feys.recruit_gift: MAN_SPEECH (utility · negotiation checks +30% while Ossory is in party)

feys.name: Morgan le Fey
feys.manifestation: not at the Faire during Nights 1-5 · Morgan appears on Night 6 ONLY, in the Big Top for the closing performance.  She takes the stage without introduction, wearing a coat of hospital-grade white and reciting a soliloquy of her own composition.  She is the closing-night boss ONLY IF the protagonist has aligned strongly unseelie by Night 5.  Otherwise she watches from the wings and does not fight.

feys.description: Sad the way people who've won several wars are sad.  Wants an heir · will not admit it.  You may qualify.  She is the ONLY tier-3 boss that cannot be recruited on a first playthrough · New Game+ only.
feys.prize: cannot be recruited on first playthrough
feys.true_name: Morgause is one of them · she has several
feys.request: cannot be promised on first playthrough
feys.recruit_gift: NG+ only · MORGAN_CURSE (Word, all-enemies massive · one use per battle)
